name: TACTILE SYSTEMS TECHNOLOGY INC
business: Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. is a medical technology company that develops and provides innovative medical devices for the treatment of underserved chronic diseases, including lymphedema, chronic venous insufficiency, and chronic respiratory conditions. It sells Flexitouch Plus, Entre Plus, and Nimbl systems for lymphedema and chronic venous insufficiency, and AffloVest for airway clearance. The company primarily sells directly to patients and providers for its lymphedema products and through durable medical equipment (DME) distributors for its respiratory product.

exposures.sentence: We are a medical technology company that develops and provides innovative medical devices for the treatment of underserved chronic diseases.

catalysts.event: Commercial launch of Nimbl for upper extremity lymphedema
catalysts.sentence: Nimbl, our next generation pneumatic compression platform, received 510(k) clearance in June 2024 and, beginning in October 2024, is commercially available throughout the United States for treatment of upper extremity lymphedema, and subsequently was commercially launched for lower extremity lymphedema in February 2025.

catalysts.event: Commercial launch of Nimbl for lower extremity lymphedema
catalysts.sentence: Nimbl, our next generation pneumatic compression platform, received 510(k) clearance in June 2024 and, beginning in October 2024, is commercially available throughout the United States for treatment of upper extremity lymphedema, and subsequently was commercially launched for lower extremity lymphedema in February 2025.

catalysts.event: Presentation of 4 and 6 month results from head and neck cancer-related lymphedema study
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the 4 and 6 month results were presented as a late breaking research poster at the ACRM 2025 102nd Annual Conference.

tamClaims.claim: Market opportunity for Flexitouch head and neck system is approximately $1 billion in the United States
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the market opportunity for our Flexitouch head and neck system is approximately $1 billion in the United States, which is based on 75% of the total number of patients suffering from cancers of the head and neck and our average selling price per device.
